name: Union-Closed Sets Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The power set P(X) of a finite ground set X, closed under arbitrary unions

VINIT.justification: The void state before any sets are collected; the initial uninitialized family F = {}

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains all possible elements; the hermetic boundary of the union-closed system

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: taking any two sets A, B ∈ F and producing A ∪ B, advancing toward closure

AREV.justification: Reverse/descent morphism: the contravariant step that descends from union back to element membership; parity flip

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: (A ∪ B) ∪ C ∪ D... — composing multiple union operations into a single closure chain

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: Identity on F: F ∪ F = F
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the family recognizes itself; union of F with itself yields F unchanged

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Partition into two branches: membership test
FSPLIT.justification: Splits the closure verification into two paths: (1) element x is in some A ∈ F, (2) element x is not in any A ∈ F

FFUSE.justification: Fuses both branches back: if x ∈ A and x ∈ B for A, B ∈ F, then x ∈ (A ∪ B) ∈ F; recovers the original closure guarantee

EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the union of any two sets in F is confirmed to be in F (closure holds)

EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the union of two sets in F is not in F (closure fails, family is not union-closed)

ENGAGR.chosen_element: Paradice: F is both union-closed and not union-closed simultaneously
ENGAGR.justification: A paradice state held without resolution: the family exhibits both closure and non-closure properties in superposition (e.g., partial closure, or undecidable membership)

IFIX.chosen_element: Permanent record: F_final (the maximal union-closed family containing F)
IFIX.justification: Irreversible fixation: once the closure is computed and recorded, it becomes append-only; no element can be removed from the final closed family

phase_2.split_input: A family F and a candidate union A ∪ B
split_outputs: T-branch: A ∪ B ∈ F (closure holds for this pair)
F-branch: A ∪ B ∉ F (closure fails for this pair)
phase_2.fuse_result: The original family F with its closure property either confirmed (T-arm) or violated (F-arm); if T-arm dominates, F remains union-closed; if F-arm dominates, F is not union-closed

phase_3.void_description: 00 — F = ∅, no sets collected, no closure property defined yet
phase_3.true_description: 01 — F is union-closed: for all A, B ∈ F, A ∪ B ∈ F
phase_3.false_description: 10 — F is not union-closed: there exist A, B ∈ F such that A ∪ B ∉ F
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice: F exhibits both closure and non-closure simultaneously (e.g., partial closure, or undecidable/superposed state)

steps.domain_action: Initialize the family F as empty; no sets exist yet, void state (00)
Establish X as the universal ground set and boundary; all unions must remain within P(X)
Add first set A to F; forward morphism toward closure
Add second set B to F; continue forward
Compose the union operation: form A ∪ B
Branch on membership test: does A ∪ B belong to F?
T-arm: A ∪ B ∈ F confirmed; closure property holds for this pair (state 01)
T-arm: add A ∪ B to F explicitly; forward morphism to extend closure
T-arm: chain further unions with existing members
F-arm: A ∪ B ∉ F; closure property violated (state 10)
F-arm: reverse morphism; descend to examine why closure failed; parity flip
F-arm: self-reference check; does F ∪ F = F? (identity on F)
Reconstitute: merge both branches; if T-arm dominates, F is union-closed; if F-arm dominates, F is not union-closed
Iterate: for all remaining pairs (C, D) in F, repeat the union and membership test
Branch again on next pair membership
T-arm: C ∪ D ∈ F; closure holds
T-arm: add C ∪ D to F
F-arm: C ∪ D ∉ F; closure fails
F-arm: reverse morphism; examine failure
Reconstitute after second pair
Continue composition through all pairs until saturation
Final self-reference: the completed family F recognizes itself; F ∪ F = F
If the domain exhibits undecidable or superposed closure (e.g., in constructive logic or quantum set theory), enter paradice state (11); both closure and non-closure held simultaneously
Permanent record: F_final is fixed as the maximal union-closed family; append-only, irreversible
Record the closure verdict: PASS (F is union-closed) or FAIL (F is not union-closed)
Seal the boundary; the system is now closed and complete

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The union-closure algorithm: iteratively applies the union operation to all pairs in F until no new sets are generated (fixed-point computation)
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Set membership queries and union operations; communication between the membership test (FSPLIT) and the closure verification (FFUSE)
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The family F is stored as a collection of sets; each set is a subset of X; the state register (00/01/10/11) tracks closure status
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Breadth-first or depth-first enumeration of all pairs (A, B) ∈ F × F; iterate until fixed point (no new sets generated)
phase_5.alfs_store: The power set P(X) and the definition of union-closure: for all A, B ∈ F, A ∪ B ∈ F

phase_6.cycle_cost: Each union operation and membership test consumes one computational step; the total cost is O(|F|²) in the worst case
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Initial family F with |F| sets; closure status unknown (void state 00)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Final family F_final with closure status determined (state 01 if closed, 10 if not closed, 11 if paradice); |F_final| ≥ |F|
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The union-closure operation is deterministic and reversible in principle: given F_final and the closure verdict, one can reconstruct the original F by removing all unions not in the original family. The system is entropy-conservative because the operation is a pure logical transformation with no information loss.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → EVALF → AREV → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → IFIX → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Union-Closed Sets Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(15,4,5,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=3
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=26
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 12), (14, 19)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑾  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑾 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [4] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [8] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [9] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [13] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [14] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [15] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [16] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [17] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [18] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [19] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [20] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [21] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [22] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [23] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑭  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [24] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑭 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [25] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def union_closed_sets_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the family F as empty; no sets exist yet, void state (00))
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Establish X as the universal ground set and boundary; all unions must remain ...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑾)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Add first set A to F; forward morphism toward closure)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Add second set B to F; continue forward)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [4] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the union operation: form A ∪ B)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch on membership test: does A ∪ B belong to F?) / FFUSE [12] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-arm: A ∪ B ∈ F confirmed; closure property holds for this pair (state 01))
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (T-arm: add A ∪ B to F explicitly; forward morphism to extend closure)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (T-arm: chain further unions with existing members)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-arm: A ∪ B ∉ F; closure property violated (state 10))
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (F-arm: reverse morphism; descend to examine why closure failed; parity flip)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (F-arm: self-reference check; does F ∪ F = F? (identity on F)))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [12]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [12] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute: merge both branches; if T-arm dominates, F is union-closed; if F-arm dominates, F is not union-closed)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑚)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Iterate: for all remaining pairs (C, D) in F, repeat the union and membership...)
  -- FSPLIT [14] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch again on next pair membership) / FFUSE [19] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [15] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T-arm: C ∪ D ∈ F; closure holds)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [16] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (T-arm: add C ∪ D to F)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [17] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (F-arm: C ∪ D ∉ F; closure fails)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [18] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (F-arm: reverse morphism; examine failure))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [19]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [19] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Reconstitute after second pair)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [20] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Continue composition through all pairs until saturation)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑳)  -- [21] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Final self-reference: the completed family F recognizes itself; F ∪ F = F)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [22] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (If the domain exhibits undecidable or superposed closure (e.g., in constructi...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [23] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Permanent record: F_final is fixed as the maximal union-closed family; append...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [24] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record the closure verdict: PASS (F is union-closed) or FAIL (F is not union-...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [25] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the boundary; the system is now closed and complete)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def union_closed_sets_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (union_closed_sets_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def union_closed_sets_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (union_closed_sets_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem union_closed_sets_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

end Imscribing
